Context: This chunk provides an overview of the Indian real estate market for February 2025, published by JLL in their 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' report. It summarizes key trends across office, retail, and residential sectors, along with significant transactions and market sentiment.

Chunk Content: ## Overview

### Office Index
Demand for office space increased in most cities

### Retail Index
Retail space take-up in shopping malls increased in most cities

### Residential Index
New launches went up in most cities

### Realestonomics
CapitaLand India Trust (CLINT) has entered into a forward purchase agreement with Maia Estates Offices Private Limited to acquire an office project at Nagawara, Outer Ring Road, Bangalore. The acquisition of the prime office project is expected to improve the earnings and distributions for Unitholders.

### Market sentiment
**Favourable**
(Legend: Favourable, Neutral, Unfavourable)

### What’s new
Arvind SmartSpaces has signed a new residential plotted development project in Ahmedabad, with a total estimated area of 150 acre and a top-line potential of nearly INR 600 cr. The project is acquired on an outright basis

### Policy update
No major policy update.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor February 2025" details the retail market dynamics across India's top seven cities for Q4 2024. It provides an overview of retail leasing activity, new supply, and international brand entries in 2024, comparing these figures to 2023.

Chunk Content: ## What’s trending in real estate
### Focus sector: Retail

**India retail market dynamics: Q4 2024**

During the calendar year 2024, 8.1 million sq. ft of area was leased by retailers in shopping malls and prominent high streets across India’s top seven cities (Mumbai, Delhi NCR, Bengaluru, Kolkata, Chennai, Pune, and Hyderabad). 2024 witnessed new retail space additions of nearly 1.7 million sq. ft across 7 retail developments. Despite limited infusion of new spaces of superior quality, momentum in retail sector’s gross leasing remained unaffected as retailers continued to expand footprint across retail formats throughout the year, with gross leasing moderating by only 6% over 2023.

Bengaluru accounted for 34% share of the total leasing activity, followed by Delhi NCR (14%), and Mumbai (14%). The concentration of 62% of total retail space take-up in these three Indian megalopolises underscores the strong consumption demand in prime retail centres and key high streets in these markets. With a significant 2.8 million sq. ft share in leasing activity, Bengaluru, alone, surpassed the combined leasing volume of Delhi-NCR and Mumbai. Notably, the share of other cities such as Hyderabad and Chennai increased from 10% and 5% of the leasing volume in 2023 to 14% and 10% at the end of 2024, respectively.

2023 witnessed a five year high as new retail supply of 6.2 million sq. ft became operational across the top 7 cities. The addition of 16 new retail developments in 2023 supported the expansion plans of retailers and helped in reaching gross leasing high of 8.7 million sq. ft in the year. However, the new supply in the retail sector in 2024 plunged by 73% y-o-y hampering the new retail space take-up by interested retailers looking for quality retail spaces. Despite this decline in new retail supply, the leasing moderated only by 6% over 2023 to 8.1 million sq. ft. Retailer appetite for prime retail locations remained firm throughout 2024 and categories such as Fashion and Apparel continued to amass the highest percentage share in the total retail leasing pie.

In 2024, 27 new brands of international origin entered India, nearly doubling from 14 in 2023. In the past four years, 60 retailers of international origin have forayed in the country to cater to Indian consumers perpetual preference for global brands. The entry of these brands in 2024 represents a 45% share of the total international brands that entered India since 2021. Beauty, Cosmetics and Wellness, Footwear, Bags and Accessories and Fashion and Apparel were the top three retailer categories to set retail footprint in the country in 2024.

**Dr. Samantak Das**
Chief Economist and Head Research & REIS, India, JLL
samantak.das@jll.com

### Focus sector: Retail

**India retail market dynamics: Q4 2024**

During the calendar year 2024, 8.1 million sq. ft of area was leased by retailers in shopping malls and prominent high streets across India’s top seven cities (Mumbai, Delhi NCR, Bengaluru, Kolkata, Chennai, Pune, and Hyderabad). 2024 witnessed new retail space additions of nearly 1.7 million sq. ft across 7 retail developments. Context: This chunk from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor February 2025" provides a "Weather map" overview of real estate market dynamics across key Indian cities. It details the current status of office rent, retail rent, residential capital value, office leasing, retail leasing, and residential launches for cities like Ahmedabad, Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, Chennai, Hyderabad, and Kolkata.

Chunk Content: ## Weather map

| Cities | Office rent | Retail rent | Residential capital value |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Ahmedabad | Stable | Stable | Stable |
| Delhi NCR | Growing | Growing | Growing |
| Mumbai | Growing | Growing | Growing |
| Pune | Growing | Growing | Growing |
| Bengaluru | Growing | Growing | Growing |
| Chennai | Stable | Growing | Growing |
| Hyderabad | Growing | Growing | Growing |
| Kolkata | Growing | Growing | Growing |

**Legend for Weather Map Table:**
*   **Growing:** Sun icon
*   **Stable:** Sun and cloud icon
*   **Stagnant:** Cloud icon
*   **Falling:** Cloud and snowflake icon

**Market Activity Indicators (Map):**
*   **Office Leasing:** Increase (Green) in Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Chennai; Stable (Orange) in Ahmedabad.
*   **Retail Leasing:** Increase (Green) in Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kolkata; Stable (Orange) in Ahmedabad, Chennai.
*   **Residential Launches:** Increase (Green) in Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kolkata; Stable (Orange) in Ahmedabad, Chennai.

Context: This chunk is from JLL's 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' for February 2025, providing a detailed overview of the real estate market in Ahmedabad. It covers city highlights, sector-specific analysis for office, retail, and residential, current market data on rental and capital values, and recent infrastructure/policy updates.

Chunk Content: ## Ahmedabad

### City highlights
Arvind SmartSpaces has acquired a new residential plotted development project in Sanand-Nalsarovar road, with a total estimated area of 150 acre and a top-line potential of INR 600 cr.

### Sector Analysis
*   **Office:** Muted demand for office space. Key transaction: Zomato at Sindhubhavan Road.
*   **Retail:** Demand in malls and Highstreet remain stagnant. Key transaction: Style Union at Nikol.
*   **Residential:** Sales and launches remain stable. Key launch: Obsidian Towers at Jagatpur.

### Major Transaction
No major transaction recorded.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental value (sq ft/month) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 45 - 65 |
| Retail | 100 - 145 |
| Residential | 25 - 45 |

**Caption:** Rental value (sq ft/month)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

| Sector | Capital Value (INR/sq ft) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 8,000 - 12,000 |
| Retail | 25,000 - 32,000 |
| Residential | 5,000 - 8,000 |

**Caption:** Capital Value (INR/sq ft)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

### Infrastructure / policy update
The Ahmedabad Urban Development Authority (AUDA) has doubled the fees for various key development plan documents, including DP and TP part plans, base maps, measurement sheets and final plot F forms.

Context: This document is JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" for February 2025, providing current real estate market dynamics across various Indian cities. This specific section details the real estate performance, key transactions, market data, and infrastructure updates for Bengaluru across office, retail, and residential sectors.

Chunk Content: ## Bengaluru

### City highlights
*   TVS Emerald has acquired 10-acre land parcel in Sathnur.
*   Morgan Stanley to set up 1.2 mn sq ft of captive campus on Outer Ring Road.
*   Strata has acquired 140,000 sq ft of office space in Whitefield for Puma Headquarters, valued at INR 115.45 Cr. The Flex space deals have been highly absorbed.
*   Reputable developers have launched new projects in prominent residential areas.

### Sector Analysis
*   **Office:** Whitefield and Outer Ring Road continue to attract a large proportion of office occupiers expanding into the city. Key transaction: HCL at Outer Ring Road.
*   **Retail:** Sustained retailer leasing in premium malls. Key transaction: Uniqlo at Rajajinagar.
*   **Residential:** Higher traction in recently launched quality projects. Key launch: Prestige Suncrest at Electronic City.

### Major Transaction
CapitaLand India Trust (CLINT) has entered into a forward purchase agreement with Maia Estate, to acquire an office project at Nagawara, Outer Ring Road.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental value (INR/ sq ft/ month) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 80 - 140 |
| Retail | 100 - 200 |
| Residential | 40 - 60 |

**Caption:** Rental value (INR/ sq ft/ month)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

| Sector | Capital value (INR/ sq ft/ month) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 12,000 - 18,000 |
| Retail | 15,000 - 25,000 |
| Residential | 12,000 - 18,000 |

**Caption:** Capital value (INR/ sq ft/ month)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

### Infrastructure / policy update
The Karnataka State Government has approved the Greater Bengaluru Integrated Satellite Township, spanning 8,032 acre between Bidadi and Harohalli, under the Greater Bangalore Development Authority (GBDA) to ease congestion in Bengaluru.

Context: This chunk provides a detailed overview of the Chennai real estate market for February 2025, extracted from JLL's 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' report. It covers city highlights, sector analysis for office, retail, and residential, major transactions, market data on rental and capital values, and infrastructure updates.

Chunk Content: ## Chennai

### City highlights
TVS Indeon Ltd., a subsidiary of Lucas TVS Ltd., inaugurated its expanded battery pack production facility at SIPCOT Industrial Park in Gummidipoondi.

### Sector Analysis
*   **Office:** Sustained leasing momentum led by BFSI and Manufacturing firms in the SBD OMR, SBD & CBD micro markets. Key transaction: Vestas at PTR.
*   **Retail:** Prominent high streets and malls continued to lead the leasing. Key transaction: GAP at Navalur.
*   **Residential:** New launches and sales activity remain muted. Key launch: Casagrand Wesley at Arumbakkam.

### Major Transaction
Eaton Electric lays foundation stone for INR 200 cr manufacturing and research facility in Sholinganallur.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 60 - 100 |
| Retail | 80 - 140 |
| Residential | 30 - 50 |

**Caption:** Rental value (INR/sq ft/month)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

| Sector | Capital value (INR/sq ft) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 10,000 - 15,000 |
| Retail | 15,000 - 22,000 |
| Residential | 8,000 - 12,000 |

**Caption:** Capital value (INR/sq ft)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

### Infrastructure / policy update
St. Thomas Mount Railway Station is undergoing an INR 14.15 cr upgrade to become a multi-modal transportation hub.

Context: This excerpt from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" for February 2025 focuses on the real estate market dynamics in Delhi NCR. It details city highlights, sector-specific analysis, major transactions, market data including rental and capital values, and infrastructure updates for the region.

Chunk Content: ## Delhi NCR

### City highlights
Indiabulls Enterprises has announced the sale of 195 units in its premium residential project, Indiabulls Estate & Club-I in Gurugram, surpassing INR 1,000 cr in sales within just 30 days of launch.

### Sector Analysis
*   **Office:** Office space take-up remained healthy. Key transaction: Allianz at Golf Course Extension Road, Gurgaon.
*   **Retail:** Strong retail leasing activity across Delhi NCR. Key transaction: True Religion at Vasant Kunj, Delhi.
*   **Residential:** Launches and sale remain strong. Key launch: Godrej Astra at Golf Course Road, Gurugram.

### Major Transaction
BPTP Group plans to invest INR 3,000 cr to develop a residential project, BPTP Amstoria Verti-Greens, in sector 102, Gurugram.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental Value (INR/ sq ft/ month) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 150 - 250 |
| Retail | 120 - 220 |
| Residential | 30 - 50 |

**Caption:** Rental Value(INR/ sq ft/ month)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

| Sector | Capital Value (INR/ sq ft) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 20,000 - 30,000 |
| Retail | 25,000 - 35,000 |
| Residential | 15,000 - 25,000 |

**Caption:** Capital Value(INR/ sq ft)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

### Infrastructure / policy update
The Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority (YEIDA) will acquire 5,000 hectare of land near the upcoming international airport at Jewar to boost the real estate in the region. The airport is likely to be operational later this year.

Context: This chunk is from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" report, published in February 2025. It provides a detailed overview of the real estate market performance, key transactions, and infrastructure updates specifically for Hyderabad during the current period.

Chunk Content: ## Hyderabad

### City highlights
CGI ISMC and ANSR leased out 1.25 lac sq ft and 1.06 lac sq ft respectively in RMZ Nexity Tower 20 in Hitec City.

### Sector Analysis
*   **Office:** Leasing momentum strongest in Hitec City submarket. Key transaction: Sanofi at Hitec City.
*   **Retail:** Prime retail locations continue to attract strong leasing interest. Key transaction: Vijayalakshmi Silks at Jubilee Hills.
*   **Residential:** Property launches and sales continue at a steady pace. Key launch: One Hyderabad Phase 2 at Somajiguda.

### Major Transaction
Telangana government and JSW UAV Ltd have signed an MoU for an INR 800 cr Unmanned Aerial Systems manufacturing facility. The project, in collaboration with a US defense technology company, will be established by the JSW Defence subsidiary.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental Value (INR/ sq ft/ month) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 60 - 90 |
| Retail | 120 - 150 |
| Residential | 30 - 50 |

**Caption:** Rental Value(INR/ sq ft/ month)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

| Sector | Capital Value (INR/ sq ft) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 8,000 - 12,000 |
| Retail | 14,000 - 18,000 |
| Residential | 8,000 - 12,000 |

**Caption:** Capital Value(INR/ sq ft)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

### Infrastructure / policy update
The Telangana government has announced the construction of the Greenfield Radial Road, named Ratan Tata Road, which will serve as a link between the Tata Interchange at Raviryal on the Outer Ring Road and the Regional Ring Road at Amangal. The project is expected to cost of INR 4,030 cr.

Chunk Content: ## Kolkata

### City highlights
Ambuja Neotia has launched a luxury residential project, Utpalaa - The Condoville with 576 units near Anandapur.

### Sector Analysis
*   **Office:** Office market witnessed strong demand. Key transaction: Smartworks at Salt Lake.
*   **Retail:** High streets & Mall reflected strong demand. Key transaction: Imagine at Jadavpur.
*   **Residential:** New launches remained strong across micro markets. Key launch: Utpalaa - The Condoville by Ambuja Neotia at Ruby.

No major transaction recorded.

No major infrastructure or policy update.



# Mumbai

**City highlights**

Gramercy Info Park Pvt Ltd secures two land plots in Ghansoli, Navi Mumbai for INR 615 cr.

**Office**
Healthy demand witnessed in Western Suburbs market.
**Key transaction:** .Firstsource Solutions at Western Suburbs.

**Retail**
Improved demand for malls and highstreets witnessed.
**Key transaction:** Decathlon at Lower Parel

**Residential**
Sale and launches remain the stable.
**Key launch:** Greenville Aster at Kasarvadavli.

WSB Real Estate Partners has allocated INR 300 cr to a newly introduced investment strategy focused on Mumbai opportunities.

Context: This document is the "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" report published by JLL in February 2025. This specific section provides an overview of the real estate market dynamics in Pune, detailing city highlights, sector-wise analysis, and market data for office, retail, and residential segments.

Chunk Content: # Pune

**City highlights**

Solitaire Group has acquired the land parcel spread over nearly 10-acre in Wagholi locality for over INR 129 cr.

**Office**
Leasing activity primarily driven by the Tech sector and Flex space providers in the Suburbs and SBD submarkets.
**Key transaction:** Table Space at Wakad.

**Retail**
Highstreet across the city has seen strong leasing activity
**Key transaction:** Zudio at Chinchwad.

**Residential**
Sales and Launches remain stable.
**Key launch:** Maan The Greenfront at Hinjewadi.

Hiranadani Group has entered into a joint development agreement with Krisala Developers to develop 105 acres of land in North Hinjewadi with an estimated revenue potential of INR 7,000 cr.

| Sector | Rental Value (INR/ sq ft/ month)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 60 - 80 |
| Retail | 100 - 140 |
| Residential | 30 - 45 |

*Table: Rental Value(INR/ sq ft/ month) in Pune. Values are shown in ranges.*

| Sector | Capital Value (INR/ sq ft)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 8,000 - 10,000 |
| Retail | 10,000 - 14,000 |
| Residential | 6,000 - 11,000 |

*Table: Capital Value(INR/ sq ft) in Pune. Values are shown in ranges.*

**Infrastructure / policy update**
No major infrastructure or policy update.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk provides an 'About JLL' section from the 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor February 2025' report, detailing JLL's global operations and its role as a leading commercial real estate and investment management company. It also clarifies that 'Pulse reports' are frequent updates on real estate market dynamics published by JLL.

Chunk Content: JLL SEE A BRIGHTER WAY

**About JLL**

For over 200 years, JLL (NYSE: JLL), a leading global commercial real estate and investment management company, has helped clients buy, build, occupy, manage and invest in a variety of commercial, industrial, hotel, residential and retail properties. A Fortune 500® company with annual revenue of $23.4 billion and operations in over 80 countries around the world, our more than 112,000 employees bring the power of a global platform combined with local expertise. Driven by our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world, we help our clients, people and communities SEE A BRIGHTER WAY<sup>SM</sup>. JLL is the brand name, and a registered trademark, of Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated. For further information, visit jll.com

Pulse reports from JLL are frequent updates on real estate market dynamics.

**Dr. Samantak Das**
Chief Economist and Head Research & REIS
+91 22 6620 7575 / +91 98198 65516
samantak.das@jll.com

14 | © 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.
